title: ESMA: A Local Nonlinear Schrödinger Framework with Informational Saturation and Collision Fidelity
author: Menezes, Jorge
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
We introduce the ESMA framework, an extension of the Schrödinger equation that incorporates locally defined nonlinear saturation mechanisms alongside a quantitative approach to wave packet interaction analysis. The model combines a logarithmic density dependent potential with a spatially localized variance operator, enabling adaptive self-confinement without reliance on global reference frames.  
  Numerical simulations, performed using the split-step Fourier method, demonstrate regimes of suppressed dispersion, coherent propagation, and high-fidelity post collision recovery. A fidelity metric is introduced to quantify structural preservation following interactions.  
  The results indicate that the system supports robust localized structures exhibiting soliton like behavior across a broad range of parameters. This work establishes a phenomenological framework for exploring nonlinear dynamics potentially associated with effective informational interactions.
steps_raw:
We introduce the ESMA framework, an extension of the Schrödinger equation that incorporates locally defined nonlinear saturation mechanisms alongside a quantitative approach to wave packet interaction analysis. The model combines a logarithmic density dependent potential with a spatially localized variance operator, enabling adaptive self-confinement without reliance on global reference frames.  
  Numerical simulations, performed using the split-step Fourier method, demonstrate regimes of suppressed dispersion, coherent propagation, and high-fidelity post collision recovery. A fidelity metric is introduced to quantify structural preservation following interactions.  
  The results indicate that the system supports robust localized structures exhibiting soliton like behavior across a broad range of parameters. This work establishes a phenomenological framework for exploring nonlinear dynamics potentially associated with effective informational interactions.
keywords: Nonlinear Schrödinger equation; Wave packet dynamics; Self confinement; Informational models; Soliton like structures; Split step Fourier method.
